narrative: Jindal Stainless remained the overall leader in FY2023-24, ranking 1st across SOx, NOx and PM. SAIL emerged as a meaningful mid-table performer where data became available, ranking 2nd on SOx and NOx, although it ranked 4th on PM. Tata improved in absolute terms on all three pollutants: SOx declined from 51 kt to 38 kt, NOx from 30 kt to 21 kt, and PM from 11 kt to 9 kt. However, Tata’s peer rank remained weak: 4th on SOx, 3rd on NOx and 3rd on PM. JSW was close to Tata on SOx and PM but weaker on NOx. For Tata, the year indicates operational improvement but insufficient relative outperformance because the peer set includes smaller EAF/stainless operations and SAIL’s lower reported SOx/NOx values. Tata FY2023-24 BRSR also notes revised FY2022-23 numbers due to boundary and methodology changes, so trend interpretation should focus on standardised values rather than raw annual-report figures.
